About

SherjeelHussain

Product Designer Ceramicist Glasgow

Designer and ceramicist. I work across physical and digital — not because I can't choose, but because both are part of how I think.

Portrait

img/sherjeel-portrait.jpg

Practice

Designer and ceramicist based in Glasgow. I trained in product design and ceramics at Gray's in Aberdeen and the two have never really separated — the thinking is the same, the materials are just different.

I grew up between two cultures and I think that comes through naturally in the work. My ceramics uses multanni mitti — a Pakistani clay from the plains of Multan — alongside British stoneware. The two clays behave completely differently at high temperature. That's the interesting part. It's not a concept, it's just what happens when you fire them together.

Background

I studied Three Dimensional Design — Product and Ceramics — at RGU in Aberdeen. In my final year I set up a Raku Club at the university. Organised the firings, sourced materials, got people along. It became an official society. It's still going, which is good.

After graduating I did a Graduate in Residence at Gray's School of Art. Spent the year mentoring students in Rhino and KeyShot, developing my own ceramics practice, and eventually curated Crafted by Design — a group show at The Courtyard Artspace in Aberdeen with eleven makers from the school. Good way to end that chapter.

Taught myself web development, brand design, and digital work by taking on real clients and figuring it out as I went. Set up SCM Design Studio in 2024. Websites, identities, signage, UI — mostly Scottish clients, all paid work.

My ceramics practice runs alongside everything else under SH27 Design. Working in multanni mitti — traditionally used in skincare across Pakistan, I started testing it in ceramics during the residency — alongside British stoneware. The xp gain has been real. Work has been shown at the Scottish Ceramics Gallery and in exhibitions in Aberdeen and Renfrewshire.

Experience

2024–25

SCM Design Studio

Founder & Lead Designer

End-to-end brand and digital projects. Websites, identities, signage, UI systems. Clients across Glasgow. Sole designer across all disciplines.

2025
Exhibition

SCOPE Collective

Cycle Arts Festival — Site-Specific Ceramics

Commissioned site-specific ceramic installations displayed along Renfrewshire cycle routes. Multiple pieces in white stoneware (Cone 9 Reduction) and flecked stoneware (Cone 6). Collaboration with Outer Spaces and local communities to integrate art with the natural landscape.

2022–23

Gray's School of Art

Graduate in Residence

Mentored students in Rhino and KeyShot. Developed personal ceramics practice. Curated Crafted by Design — independent group exhibition at The Courtyard Artspace, Aberdeen. Managed studio operations and kiln firings.

2023
Exhibition

The Courtyard Artspace, Aberdeen

Crafted by Design — Curator & Organiser

Group exhibition showcasing emergent designers and artists from Gray's School of Art. End-to-end delivery: artist coordination, spatial planning, installation, social promotion. One month preparation, two-week run.

2022–23
Exhibition

Gray's School of Art

BLEND — GiR Program Exhibition

Group exhibition as part of the Graduate in Residence programme. Presented ceramics and product work alongside fellow GiR participants.

2022

TWOSIX Design Studio

Product Designer Intern

Additive manufacturing and rapid prototyping. Secured and delivered a ceramic tableware client — 40 units in one month via slip casting.

2021

Origin Plastics

Product Designer Intern

Homeware product design using recycled injection-moulded plastic. CAD, renders, user research with 100+ participants. Sustainable manufacturing focus.

2021
Initiative

Robert Gordon University

Raku Club — Founder

Founded an informal Raku firing club that grew into an officially recognised University Society. Organised outdoor firings, sourced materials, built the community from scratch. The society still runs.

2018–22

The Robert Gordon University

BA(Hons) Three Dimensional Design — Product + Ceramics

2:1. Honoured project exhibited at New Designers, London. Founded Raku Club. Social Media Secretary, Weightlifting Society.

Let's makesomething.

Open to full-time roles in product design, UI design, or brand — and studio or residency opportunities. Remote-first, willing to relocate for the right position.
